- Add 'export' to Command enum
- ExportArgs struct with all filter fields
- parse_export_args() function

- run_export() function:
  1. Open SQLite (error if missing)
  2. Build query with WHERE clause from filters
  3. Stream rows to stdout in requested format
  4. Handle Ctrl+C gracefully (don't corrupt output)

  - Wrap fields with commas/quotes/newlines in quotes
  - Join with comma, output CRLF

- format_jsonl_row(row) function:

- Detect and highlight reconnection patterns (same remote, repeated connect/close cycles)
- Surface rate limiting, network issues, or bugs in AI clients

- See warning: '⚠ Retry pattern: 5 connections to api.anthropic.com:443 in 30s'
- Identify problematic connection behavior without manual analysis
- Correlate retries with provider responses

- Track connection events per (remote_ip, remote_port, pid)
- Configurable threshold: --retry-threshold <N> (default 3)
- Configurable window: --retry-window-ms <N> (default 60000)

- Store retry_count in SQLite for analysis

Implementation:
- HashMap<(IpAddr, u16, u32), Vec<Instant>> for recent connections
- On close event: check if count in window exceeds threshold
- Prune old entries on check (sliding window)

 {"id":"bd-1jj","title":"Design alert system architecture","description":"Deliverables:
- AlertConfig struct with all threshold fields
- Decision on pattern matching library (regex vs glob vs both)
- Define alert event structure for SQLite storage
- Define alert output format (stderr, prefix, colors)
- Decide on alert state tracking (cooldown to avoid spam)

Design questions to answer:
1. Should alerts be per-connection or per-threshold-breach?
2. How to handle alert cooldown (same alert within N seconds)?
3. Should we track 'alert acknowledged' state?
4. How do alerts interact with --json output mode?

Output:
- Design doc in docs/alert-design.md
- Updated schema proposal for events table","design":"Design decisions (pre-answered to speed implementation):
1. Alerts should be per-connection (each matching event triggers alert, subject to cooldown)
2. Cooldown: HashMap<AlertType, Instant> - skip alert if same type within cooldown_ms
3. No 'acknowledged' state for v1 - keep it simple, user handles alerts
4. JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with alert:true field, same structure as events

Pattern matching approach:
- Use glob for simple wildcards (most common case)
- Allow regex with r: prefix for power users (e.g., 'r:.*evil.*')
- Default to glob if no prefix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.505557159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195422658Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195219435Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jj","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.527150866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ju","title":"Process ancestry chain display","description":"Purpose:
- Show full process tree path for spawned AI processes
- Enable understanding of multi-layer orchestration (shell → tmux → agent → claude)
- Critical for debugging and auditing agent swarms

User impact:
- 'rano --show-ancestry' displays: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → bash(789) → claude(1234)'
- Users understand exactly how AI processes were spawned
- Helps identify rogue or unexpected process spawning

Features:
- --show-ancestry flag: include ancestry in event output
- Ancestry format: 'parent_comm(pid) → ... → current_comm(pid)'
- Cache ancestry to avoid repeated /proc walks
- Store ancestry_path in SQLite for historical queries
- Report includes ancestry breakdown

Implementation approach:
- read_ancestry(pid) walks /proc/{pid}/stat PPID chain to init
- Cache: HashMap<pid, Vec<(pid, String)>> with TTL
- Ancestry invalidated if PID recycled (check comm matches)

Testing:
- Unit tests for ancestry chain parsing
- E2E script with nested process tree (shell → script → rano)
- Verify ancestry displayed correctly in output and stored in SQLite","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:18.765913116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:31.933812417Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:31.933767403Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: ancestry chain reading (bd-vb7), CLI flag + SQLite storage (bd-15l), and tests + docs (bd-94z).  {"id":"bd-2a6","title":"Design export format and CLI","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI design document:
  rano export [OPTIONS]
  --format csv|jsonl (required)
  --sqlite <path> (default: observer.sqlite)
  --since <timestamp|relative> (e.g., 24h, 7d, 2026-01-20)
  --until <timestamp|relative>
  --run-id <id>
  --provider <name> (repeatable)
  --domain <pattern> (repeatable)
  --fields <list> (optional: override default field list)
  --no-header (CSV only: skip header row)

- Define field order and names:
  ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto, local_ip, local_port, remote_ip, remote_port, domain, duration_ms

- CSV format spec:
  - RFC 4180 compliant
  - Fields with commas/quotes/newlines properly escaped
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - CRLF line endings (for Excel compatibility)

- JSONL format spec:
  - One JSON object per line
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - LF line endings
  - Same fields as CSV

- Output: docs/export-design.md","design":"Implementation notes:
- CSV escaping: double quotes to escape quotes, wrap fields containing comma/quote/newline
- No external CSV crate needed - simple escaping logic
- Streaming: use SQLite row iteration with step(), don't collect()
- Memory: flush stdout every 1000 rows
- Empty result: CSV outputs header only, JSONL outputs nothing
- Progress: no progress bar (would interfere with piping)
- Errors: fail fast on invalid filter, print to stderr

Query template:
SELECT ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto,
       local_ip, local_port, remote_ip, remote_port, domain, duration_ms
FROM events
WHERE 1=1
  [AND ts >= :since]
  [AND ts < :until]
  [AND run_id = :run_id]
  [AND provider IN (:providers)]
  [AND domain LIKE :domain_pattern]
ORDER BY ts ASC","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.336206689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:57.416472667Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:57.416429906Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2a6","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.364391899Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2iu","title":"Alert thresholds and watch mode","description":"Purpose:
- Transform passive monitoring into active alerting
- Allow users to set guard rails and not watch output constantly
- Enable security-focused workflows with domain watching

User impact:
- 'rano --alert-domain evil.com --alert-max-connections 100' instantly flags suspicious activity
- Auditors can set thresholds and walk away
- Alerts are prominent and logged separately

Features:
- --alert-domain <pattern>: Regex/glob for domains to highlight/warn
- --alert-max-connections <N>: Warn when total connections exceed N
- --alert-max-per-provider <N>: Warn when any provider exceeds N
- --alert-duration-ms <N>: Flag connections lasting longer than N ms
- --alert-unknown-domain: Warn on any connection to unresolved domain

Output behavior:
- Alerts print to stderr with [ALERT] prefix
- Alerts use distinct color (red/yellow)
- Optional --alert-bell to ring terminal bell
- Alert events logged to SQLite with alert=true flag

Testing:
- Unit tests for threshold checking and pattern matching
- E2E script that triggers each alert type and validates output
- E2E script must verify alerts don't fire for normal traffic

Constraints:
- Must not add latency to main polling loop
- Patterns must be case-insensitive and support wildcards","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:19.697313595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:37:55.697417500Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:37:55.697362296Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2iu","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:19.713686680Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-db8","title":"Design preset system","description":"Deliverables:
- Preset specification:
  - File format: same key=value as config.conf
  - File location: ~/.config/rano/presets/<name>.conf
  - Precedence: CLI > preset > config.conf > defaults

- Define built-in presets (embedded in binary):
  - audit.conf: for security auditing
  - quiet.conf: minimal output
  - live.conf: real-time monitoring focus
  - verbose.conf: maximum detail

- CLI design:
  - --preset <name>: load named preset
  - --list-presets: show available presets with descriptions
  - Multiple --preset flags: merge in order (later wins)

- Error handling:

 {"id":"bd-vb7","title":"Implement ancestry chain reading","description":"Deliverables:
- read_ancestry(pid) -> Vec<(u32, String)>:
  - Walk /proc/{pid}/stat to get PPID
  - Recursively read parent until PPID=0 or PPID=1
  - Return chain from root to current: [(init_pid, init_comm), ..., (pid, comm)]

- AncestryCache struct:
  - cache: HashMap<u32, (Vec<(u32, String)>, Instant)>
  - ttl: Duration (default 30s)
  - get_or_compute(pid) method with staleness check

- Staleness detection:
  - If cached entry exists, verify current comm matches cached comm
  - If mismatch (PID recycled), invalidate and recompute

- format_ancestry(chain) -> String:
  - Output: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → claude(789)'
  - Truncate if >5 levels: '... → parent(456) → claude(789)'
  - Handle single-level: 'claude(789)'

- Integration:
  - Add ancestry_cache to main state
  - Lookup ancestry when --show-ancestry enabled
  - Pass ancestry to event formatting","design":"Implementation approach:
- Reuse existing read_ppid() from descendant tracking
- Stack-based iteration (not recursion) to avoid stack overflow
- Stop at PPID=0 (kernel) or PPID=1 (init/systemd)
- Reverse to get root→leaf order

Performance considerations:
- Cache is essential - ancestry changes rarely
- 30s TTL is reasonable (processes don't typically reparent)
- Staleness check: comm is sufficient (PID reuse with same comm is rare)
- Memory: ~100 bytes per cached entry, max ~1000 tracked PIDs = 100KB

Edge cases:
- Process exits during walk: return partial chain with '?' for missing
- Zombie process: use comm from cmdline fallback
- Init containers: stop at container init, not host init","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.224824805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313186516Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313142694Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.294596381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-vb7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented ancestry chain reading + cache in src/main.rs (read_ancestry, AncestryCache w/ TTL + PID reuse check, format_ancestry truncation). Integrated optional ancestry output into connect/close event formatting (pretty + JSON) gated by args.show_ancestry. Added show_ancestry field in MonitorArgs. cargo test -q passed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:10:58Z"}]}
-{"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","title":"rano vNext: accuracy + insight + performance","description":"Goals:
- Make domain attribution accurate and trustworthy when possible.
- Make SQLite logging obviously useful through reporting and live insights.
- Improve performance and reliability under high event rates.
- Keep UX simple: defaults work, advanced options are discoverable.

Context:
- Current capture is PTR-only and can misattribute domains.
- SQLite has strong schema/views but no first-class reporting UX.
- Event rates can spike and cause sqlite overhead.
- Provider mapping is fixed to substring matching; users need overrides.

Scope:
- Implement 5 prioritized improvements with tasks, tests, and docs.
- Preserve existing CLI behavior; improvements are additive.

Quality bar:
- Every feature must include comprehensive unit tests and at least one e2e test script.
- E2E scripts must include detailed, human-readable logging (inputs, expected outputs, actual outputs).
- Tests must run offline without elevated privileges unless explicitly marked otherwise.

Acceptance:
- Each child epic has tasks with unit tests + e2e scripts + docs.
- All new features degrade gracefully (no sudo/libpcap, missing config).

-{"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","title":"True domain attribution via libpcap DNS+SNI","description":"Purpose:
- Provide accurate hostname attribution via DNS responses and TLS SNI, not just PTR.
- Preserve current PTR fallback when libpcap/sudo is unavailable.

User impact:
- Domains match what the client intended (SNI), not a reverse DNS guess.
- Clear warnings when privileged capture is unavailable; no silent failure.

Constraints:
- Must be feature-gated to avoid new deps for default builds.
- Must avoid any breaking change to default CLI behavior.

Testing:
- Unit tests for DNS/SNI parsing + mapping logic.
- E2E script using offline pcap fixtures to validate domain attribution and fallback logging.

","design":"Stats views + CLI flag design\n\nFlags\n- --stats-view <provider|domain|port|process> (repeatable).\n  - If omitted: default view list = [provider].\n  - If provided multiple times: cycle only those views (preserve order).\n- --stats-cycle-ms <ms> (default 0/off). When >0 and stats enabled, rotate views at this interval.\n- Existing --stats-interval-ms controls refresh cadence; cycle is independent (switch view on next refresh after cycle elapses).\n\nTop-N + formatting\n- Reuse --stats-top (default 5) for domain/port/process lists.\n- Provider view shows all providers with bars.\n- Domain view: top domains by count; include provider aggregate counts in parentheses if available.\n- Port view: top remote ports by count; optionally show proto.\n- Process view: top comm names by count.\n\nTerminal width / TTY behavior\n- If stdout is TTY: compute bar width from --stats-width if set, else from COLUMNS (clamped 20..80).\n- If stdout is NOT TTY: suppress live stats by default; only print final summary (current behavior) or emit compact one-line stats per interval if --stats-interval-ms > 0 and --summary-only=false.\n- When --json is set: disable live stats output regardless of view/cycle.\n\nNotes\n- Views should render as distinct sections with a short header (e.g., "Live Stats [domain]").\n- Cycling should be no-op if stats interval is 0 or summary-only is enabled.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:00:48.264069984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:05:08.895516210Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:05:08.895516210Z","close_reason":"Closed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5.1","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:55:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
